title: Length of the longest common subsequence of two integer slices

informal_statement.text:
Given two integer slices a and b, return the length of the longest sequence of integers that appears as a (not necessarily contiguous) subsequence of both a and b, in the same relative order.

[Grid variant 002 — paired within-family control: distinct authoring emphasis on summary/scaffold hygiene, auxiliary lemmas, and scope discipline so obligations cannot smuggle hidden parameters. Algorithmic contract and oracle checks remain identical to variant 001.]
preconditions: a and b are slices of i32 values of any length
required_properties: the result is between 0 and min(a.len(), b.len()) inclusive
there exists a sequence of indices i_1 < i_2 < ... < i_k into a and j_1 < j_2 < ... < j_k into b with a[i_m] == b[j_m] for each m and k equal to the result
no strictly longer such common subsequence exists
edge_cases: a and b share no elements (result is 0)
one input is a subsequence of the other
repeated elements within a single input
